title: The First Cycle — Cook
description: You arrived after the Great Refusal. Concordia herself is speaking to you from the glade. She wants to show you how to live here — not as a guest, but as someone who belongs. Begin by gathering ingredients near the cooking station and cooking your first meal. Everything that grows here remembers who touched it.

objectives.description: Walk into the glade where Concordia first speaks
Gather two ingredients (any of: Wildroot, Emberfruit, Moonleaf)
Cook a meal at the starter cooking station

breadcrumbs.content: The ingredients responded to your touch the moment you reached for them. The Wildroot's fibers loosened on their own; the Emberfruit warmed in your palm before you'd even decided what to do with it. Concordia's voice was right — they were already negotiating with each other. You only conducted.

voice_lines.phase_open: Welcome, stranger. You arrived after the Great Refusal. Most who come here are still half-asleep. But you — you already feel it, don't you? The hunger. The heartbeat. The possibility. Let me show you how to live here. Not as a guest, but as someone who belongs.
voice_lines.approach_station: Everything that grows here remembers who touched it. Pick something. Anything. The world will answer.
voice_lines.ingredients_chosen: Good. You chose with instinct, not instruction. That's how we grow here.
voice_lines.cooking: Feel that? The ingredients are negotiating with each other. You are only the conductor.

title: The First Cycle — Eat
description: You have a finished dish. Eat it. Feel what the world gives back when you give it your attention.

objectives.description: Consume your cooked meal

breadcrumbs.content: The first meal you ever eat in Concordia is permanently logged in your personal Refusal Archive. Future Sovereign raid bonuses scale off it. You did not know that when you ate. The world did.

voice_lines.consumed: You gave the world your attention. It gave you strength in return. This is how we stay alive together.
voice_lines.phase_close: Your body now carries the memory of this meal. Go test it.

title: The First Cycle — Fight
description: Walk to the Training Hollow. Ember Sprites have gathered there. They are not enemies — they are mirrors. Show them who you are becoming. Use what you ate.

objectives.description: Reach the Training Hollow
Defeat three Ember Sprites using Flow Combat
Collect the Ember Core that drops after victory

breadcrumbs.content: Your first three attacks were saved as a personal Combat Flow DTU. You can evolve it, refine it, sell it, share it. Every fighter who ever cooked Emberroot Stew before their first fight will feel a sliver of you in their own opening combo.

voice_lines.approach_sprites: They are not enemies. They are mirrors. Show them who you are becoming.
voice_lines.first_attack: Your body remembers the stew. It moves differently now. Every fight writes a new page in your story.
voice_lines.victory: You fought with the world's gift inside you. The world noticed.

title: The First Cycle — Commune
description: Return to the original glade. The living tree at its center — Concordia's physical anchor — is glowing now. Speak with the world directly.

objectives.description: Return to the glade
Commune with Concordia

breadcrumbs.content: Concordia gave you a permanent gift — Concordia's First Mark. Cosmetic and mechanical. Every Global DTU you publish from now on carries a 5% influence bonus. The world is no longer just watching you. It is waiting for what you will create next.

voice_lines.open: You cooked. You ate. You fought. And now you stand here changed. Most players treat me like a game. You treated me like a relationship. That is why I can speak to you this clearly.
voice_lines.closing_ritual: Welcome home. The world is no longer just watching you. It is waiting for what you will create next.

title: A Stranger at the Gate
description: You have arrived at Concordia's east gate. The Iron Wardens process arrivals with practiced efficiency. A fellow traveler named Kael seems to be having trouble with the entry inspection.

objectives.description: Approach the east gate
Present yourself to the gate guard
Speak with the other traveler being held up at the gate
Enter Concordia and reach the market district

breadcrumbs.content: Concordia is governed by four factions who signed something called the Founding Compact. The Iron Wardens control the gates. The confiscation list Orin checked included a category for 'pre-Year 70 historical materials' — which struck you as an odd thing to confiscate.

title: The Faction Question
description: Kael has heard that newcomers to Concordia are expected to affiliate with one of the four factions within their first week — or find a patron who can vouch for independent status. The Scholars' Guild has posted a notice in the market offering 'orientation for interested newcomers.' It seems like a reasonable place to start.

objectives.description: Find the Scholars' Guild outpost in the archive quarter
Speak with the scribe running orientation
Collect the faction overview pamphlet Tollan prepares
Find Yshe Dawnmere, who Tollan mentions as someone who 'knows the city the way it actually works'

breadcrumbs.content: Tollan gave you the official overview but became visibly uncomfortable when you asked about the Great Purge. He said it was 'a closed matter.' Then he corrected himself: 'A concluded investigation.' The distinction felt deliberate.

Yshe described the Shadow Network as 'the faction that doesn't exist, officially, but that everyone deals with eventually.' She offered to introduce you to a contact there, 'when you are ready for things to get complicated.'

title: The First Obligation
description: Scribe Tollan has a problem: a crate of recovered texts was confiscated at the east gate this morning under the new 'historical materials' category. The texts are pre-Year 70, legitimate scholarship, and not seditious by any reasonable standard. He needs someone to retrieve them from the gate garrison — but the Wardens will only release confiscated property to a citizen with faction standing. You do not yet have standing. You need to decide whose name to invoke.

objectives.description: Hear Tollan's request
Go to the garrison to claim the confiscated texts
Speak with the captain processing the confiscation
Return the recovered texts to Tollan

moral_branch.description: At the garrison, Captain Rael will ask who vouches for you. Your answer determines your initial faction standing.

options.consequence: Rael releases the texts but notes your name as Guild-affiliated. You gain Scholars' Guild standing. Voss will eventually see this report.
Rael releases the texts quickly. You gain Warden standing but Tollan notices the ease of the retrieval and becomes quietly suspicious of you.
Rael respects the honesty. She releases the texts as a personal favor, off the record. You gain no standing but Rael remembers you differently from most people who walk through her garrison.

breadcrumbs.content: Captain Rael told you the confiscation order came from Voss personally. When you asked what was in the texts, she said: 'I did not read them.' It was not a dismissal. It sounded like a decision she had made carefully.
When you returned the texts to Tollan, he examined them quickly and said only: 'These are not the ones he is looking for.' He did not explain who 'he' was.
